provisions.heading_context_raw: Requirements Coastal Areas
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 74(2), 74(3), 74(4), and 74(6), where a lot abuts the coast of the Atlantic Ocean, including its inlets, bays, and harbours, a development permit shall not be issued for any portion of a main building or a backyard suite use, including their basements, that are proposed to be erected, constructed, altered, reconstructed, or located at an elevation less than 3.2 metres above the Canadian Geodetic Vertical Datum 2013 (CGVD2013) standard (Diagram 2) for any:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Requirements Coastal Areas
provisions.text: residential use listed in Tables 1A, 1B, 1C, or 1D;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Requirements Coastal Areas
provisions.text: commercial use listed in Tables 1A, 1B, 1C, or 1D; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Requirements Coastal Areas
provisions.text: institutional use listed in Tables 1A, 1B, 1C, or 1D. Diagram 2: Coastal area elevation requirements, per Subsection 74(1)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Requirements Coastal Areas
provisions.text: Any main building located in an HRI or DND zone shall be exempt from the requirements of Subsection 74(1).

provisions.heading_context_raw: Requirements Coastal Areas
provisions.text: A development permit may be issued for a parking lot, parking structure use, underground parking structure, amenity space, or a storage space permitted in this By-law that is:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Requirements Coastal Areas
provisions.text: accessory to a main building; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Requirements Coastal Areas
provisions.text: proposed to be erected, constructed, altered, reconstructed, or located at an elevation less than 3.2 metres above the Canadian Geodetic Vertical Datum 2013 (CGVD2013) standard.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Requirements Coastal Areas
provisions.text: Within the Dartmouth Waterfront (DW) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3A, and the Halifax Waterfront (HW) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3B, a development permit may be issued for any commercial or institutional use listed in Tables 1A, 1B, 1C, or 1D, which is located at an elevation less than 3.2 metres above the Canadian Geodetic Vertical Datum 2013

provisions.heading_context_raw: Requirements Coastal Areas
provisions.text: standard (Diagram 2), provided a report by a professional engineer with an Infrastructure Resilience Professional designation is submitted to the Development Officer identifying measures that will be used to mitigate risks of flood damage and public safety on the development site, including confirmation that the proposed development does not increase the risk or hazard for surrounding buildings, adjacent uses, or adjacent coastlines.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Requirements Coastal Areas
provisions.text: Prior to issuance of the first occupancy permit, a report from a professional engineer with an Infrastructure Resilience Professional designation shall be submitted certifying that all mitigation measures identified in the report submitted under Subsection 74(4) are completed.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Requirements Coastal Areas
provisions.text: Any portion of a main building or a backyard suite use, including below grade portions, located lower than the elevation required in Subsection 74(1) that existed on the coming into force date of this By-law, may be expanded if such expansion does not increase the footprint of any portion of the main building or backyard suite use located lower than the elevation required in Subsection 74(1) by more than 25%, or further reduce the existing elevation of the main building or the backyard suite use.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Requirements Coastal Areas
provisions.text: Before issuing a development permit, the Development Officer may require a site plan certified by a surveyor or professional engineer that is drawn to scale and shows land contours and lot grading information, to determine if a main building or a backyard suite use that is being proposed to be erected, constructed, altered, reconstructed, or located on a lot that abuts the coast of the Atlantic Ocean meets the requirements of this Section. Watercourse Buffers

sections.title_label_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax Harbour", as shown on Schedule 10.

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: A development permit shall not be issued for any development within 20 metres of the ordinary high water mark of any watercourse (Diagram 3). Diagram 3: Minimum required watercourse buffer, per Subsection 75(2)

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: Where the average slope of land within the minimum required 20-metre watercourse buffer exceeds 20%, the buffer width shall be increased by 1.0 metre for each additional 2% of slope above 20%, to a maximum of 60 metres.

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: Where a wetland and a watercourse are contiguous, the watercourse buffer shall be applied from the outermost boundary of the wetland and watercourse.

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 75(6) and 75(9), within the watercourse buffer required in Subsections 75(2), 75(3), and 75(4), no excavation, infilling, or the removal of any tree, stump, or other vegetation, nor any other change, is permitted.

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: Within the watercourse buffer required in Subsections 75(2), 75(3), and 75(4), vegetation may be removed to permit the following activities:

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: subject to Subsection 75(7), one accessory structure, including a boathouse, and one attached uncovered balcony or patio, occupying a maximum of 20.0 square metres, combined;

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: boardwalks, walkways, trails, and driveways not exceeding 3.0 metres in width;

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: fences;

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: water access structure uses, boat ramps, marine-related uses, parks on public land, and historic site or monument uses;

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: streets; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: water, wastewater, and stormwater infrastructure.

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: Where a main building existed on the coming into force date of this By-law and is located within a required watercourse buffer, accessory structures permitted in Clause 75(6)(a) shall not be located any closer to the watercourse than the main building that existed on the coming into force date of this By-law.

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: Where the configuration of a lot is such that no main building can be located on the lot, the watercourse buffer distance required in Subsections 75(2), 75(3), and 75(4) may be reduced in a manner that would provide the greatest possible separation from a watercourse, if the other setback distance requirements are met, for lots that:

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: existed before August 26, 2006; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: were approved as a result of a tentative or final subdivision application on file before August 26, 2006.

provisions.heading_context_raw: This Section does not apply to any lands designated "Halifax
provisions.text: Within a required watercourse buffer, the Development Officer may authorize the removal of windblown, diseased, or dead trees that are deemed to be hazardous or unsafe to persons or property, or the selective removal of vegetation to maintain the overall health of the buffer, if a management plan is submitted by a qualified professional. provisions.text: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3E, the reference line of the Northwest Arm is as shown on Schedule 12.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: Subject to Subsections 76(3) and 76(4), a structure shall not be located within 9.0 metres of the reference line of the Northwest Arm, as shown on Schedule 12, except:

provisions.text: public infrastructure;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: utility uses;

provisions.text: boathouses;

provisions.text: publicly owned or operated ferry terminals;

provisions.text: parks on public land;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: water access structures;

provisions.text: gazebos;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: municipal, provincial, and national historic sites and monuments;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: retaining walls; and (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: staircases. (RCCC-Sep 4/24;E-Apr 17/25)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: Subject to Subsection 76(4), a lot is limited to a maximum of one boathouse and one gazebo located within 9.0 metres of the reference line of the Northwest Arm, as shown on Schedule 12, with each boathouse or gazebo not to exceed:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: a footprint of 48 square metres;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: a width of 6.0 metres on the side that is most parallel to the reference line;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: a length of 8.0 metres; and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: a height of 4.2 metres.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: The requirements of Subsections 76(2) and 76(3) do not apply within the Boat Clubs (BC) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3E.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: Water lot infilling, in whole or in part, of a water lot in the Northwest Arm Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3E, beyond the reference line of the Northwest Arm, as shown on Schedule 12, is prohibited unless a development permit has been issued for a permitted use listed in Subsection 76(4.2). (RC-Jan 9/24;E-Feb 12/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: Water lot infilling, in whole or in part, of a water lot in the Northwest Arm Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3E, beyond the reference line of the Northwest Arm, as shown on Schedule 12, is only permitted for:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: public infrastructure;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: utility uses;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: publicly owned or operated ferry terminals;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: parks on public land;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: municipal, provincial, and national historic sites and monuments;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: retaining walls associated with any uses referenced in Clauses 76(4.2)(a) to 76(4.2)(e); and

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: subject to Subsection 76(4.3), retaining walls not associated with uses referenced in Clauses 76(4.2)(a) to 76(4.2)(e). (RC-Jan 9/24;E-Feb 12/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: Retaining walls under Clause 76(4.2)(g), including any associated engineering support structure, shall not be located more than 2.0 metres beyond the reference line of the Northwest Arm, as shown on Schedule 12. (RC-Jan 9/24;E-Feb 12/24)

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: Any portion of a water lot that has been infilled beyond the reference line of the Northwest Arm, as shown on Schedule 12, shall not be included in the:

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: calculation of the minimum lot area requirements of Part IV;

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: calculation of lot coverage; or

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: measurement of a side or rear setback.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Northwest Arm (NWA) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: The requirements of this Section continue to apply regardless of whether a water lot is consolidated with an abutting land lot. Dartmouth Cove (DC) Special Area

provisions.text: Within the Dartmouth Cove (DC) Special Area, as shown on Schedule 3E, the reference line of Dartmouth Cove is as shown on Schedule 53.

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Dartmouth Cove (DC) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: Water lot infilling, in whole or in part, of a water lot as shown on Schedule 54 is prohibited, unless a development permit has been issued for a permitted use listed in Subsection 76.5(4), other than the use specified in Clause 76.5(4)(h).

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Dartmouth Cove (DC) Special Area, as shown on

provisions.heading_context_raw: Within the Dartmouth Cove (DC) Special Area, as shown on
provisions.text: Water lot infilling, in whole or in part, of a water lot, as shown on Schedule 53, is only permitted for:

sections.title_label_raw: All development is prohibited within any wetland, as shown on

provisions.heading_context_raw: All development is prohibited within any wetland, as shown on
provisions.text: All development is prohibited within any wetland, as shown on Schedule 11, unless an approval that permits the alteration or infilling of a wetland has been received from the Province of Nova Scotia.
